ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Continued To May-05-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alyssa V. Daatio for and with Craig Martinez, Petitioner (via Zoom).
The following grandchildren and great-grandchildren of the decedent were present in court: Carlita Fuentes, Cora Fuentes, Laura Martinez, Esperanza Fuentes, and Paul Fuentes.

Counsel explained that the need for probate was only recently discovered after family discussions revealed that title to the sole asset, a San Francisco property, remained in the decedent's name for the past 24 years. The petition was filed to clear title and facilitate the proper transfer of ownership. No objections were raised by the family members present in court, but Carlita Fuentes requested that Ms. Daatio consult with their real estate broker (Robert Reyes) to facilitate a resolution. The court facilitated the exchange of the broker's contact information.

Ms. Daatio requested waiver of bond and limited IAEA authority. The court found this appropriate given that the only asset in the estate is real property.

There being no objections, the court granted the Petition for Lost Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The lost will dated July 26, 1977, is admitted to probate. Craig Martinez is appointed administrator with will annexed of decedent's will with limited IAEA authority. Bond is waived. Shirey Yawitz is appointed probate referee. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for April 7, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either is petition is filed two weeks prior the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
